scope: Debug-only investigation of pi-worker-watch silent worker behavior. No source code was edited.

watcher_behavior.status_write: run-worker.sh writes <run-dir>/status only after pi returns
watcher_behavior.log_init: pi-worker-watch.sh truncates output.log to zero bytes before starting pi
watcher_behavior.watch_loop: watch_run waits for a non-empty status file, otherwise reports live tmux session and tails output.log

completed_prior_workers.status: All inspected pre-MVP workers had status file content 0 and non-empty output.log summaries.

current_watch_result.result: reported worker still running and tailed an empty output.log

findings.finding: An empty output.log while a worker is still running is expected under this watcher design if pi -p emits only final stdout/stderr at completion.
evidence: The generated runner redirects all pi stdout/stderr to output.log.
The watcher only tails that file; it does not observe model progress, internal session state, or tool events.
Earlier completed workers' logs contain only final completion summaries, consistent with no useful incremental log output.

findings.finding: The three older MVP run directories are not live workers; they are incomplete run records with no status file and no tmux session.
evidence: Their output.log files are zero bytes and their status files are missing.

Because run-worker.sh has set -u, not set -e, a normal nonzero pi exit should still write status. Missing status is more consistent with the tmux session or runner being killed before the post-pi status write.

findings.finding: There is no direct evidence from allowed files that the watcher script itself dropped output from a completed pi process.
evidence: All completed inspected workers have non-empty logs and status=0.
The live worker has no status yet, so output.log emptiness cannot distinguish slow/hung model execution from normal non-streaming operation.

findings.finding: Duplicate starts likely contributed to confusion: the same task pointer was launched more than once while previous attempts had not produced a terminal status artifact.
evidence: Only the latest MVP-005 session is currently live.

likely_cause: The apparent silence is primarily a logging/observability property of pi-worker-watch.sh plus pi -p: output.log starts empty and may remain empty until pi exits. Separately, the older empty/no-status MVP run dirs appear to be aborted tmux/runner sessions, not successful silent completions.
uncertainty: I did not inspect pi internals or pi session files, so I cannot prove whether the current pi process is actively waiting on a model, blocked on provider authentication/network, or doing useful work.
I did not read task bodies because the requested allowed-read set was narrower than the task files themselves.
I did not run a minimal model-backed pi invocation because the current evidence was sufficient and an extra invocation could create more live pi activity.

recommended_next_commands.purpose: Poll the only currently live worker without starting another duplicate.
Verify whether the live worker still has the expected tmux, runner, and pi process chain.
List worker tmux sessions before deciding whether to rerun or abandon any work.
If the live worker disappears without a status file, treat the run as aborted and rerun the task once, not in parallel with duplicates.

do_not_do_next: Do not start additional duplicate workers for MVP-005 while pi-worker-20260528-222722-354882-6966 is still live.
Do not interpret zero-byte output.log alone as failure while status is missing and tmux still has the session.
Do not rely on old no-status run directories as completed handoffs.
